footer.disclaimer: Drug information is intended for educational purposes only. Consult medical professionals for treatment decisions.
footer.disclaimerFull: OncoInfo is not a medical device and is not regulated under the MDR (Medical Device Regulation). The information provided is intended solely as an educational and informational reference for healthcare professionals. Content may contain errors, omissions, or outdated data. Never use this information as the sole basis for clinical decisions. Always consult the official product information (SmPC) and current guidelines. The developers accept no liability for any damage resulting from the use of this application.

manual.title: 📖 User Manual
manual.subtitle: Welcome to OncoInfo — your digital drug library for oncological therapies. Below you will find an overview of all features.
manual.s1Intro: Open OncoInfo in your browser (desktop, tablet or smartphone).
manual.s1Step1: Select your hospital from the dropdown menu.
manual.s1Tip: Your last selected hospital is automatically remembered for your next visit. At the bottom of the login page you can change the interface language (🇳🇱 🇫🇷 🇩🇪 🇬🇧).
manual.s1FirstLogin: After your first login you will be asked to change your password. This is required for the security of your account.
manual.s2Intro: After logging in you will arrive at the home page. Here you will find:
manual.s2MostUsedDesc: At the top you will see your personal shortcuts — up to 8 drugs you have marked as "most used". Click a shortcut to navigate directly to the drug.
manual.s2SpecialtiesDesc: Choose an oncological specialty to view the associated drugs:
manual.s2SpecialtiesLock: Specialties not activated for your hospital are greyed out and not clickable.
manual.s2SearchDesc: At the bottom of the home page you can search for a drug by name or brand name. Results appear instantly as you type (from 2 characters).
manual.s3Intro: After choosing a specialty you will see all available drugs, divided into two sections:
manual.s3FiltersDesc: Use the filter bar at the top to filter by:
manual.s3FilterSubtype: Subtype — e.g. HR+, HER2+, TNBC (for breast cancer)
manual.s3FilterArea: Disease area — e.g. Prostate cancer, Bladder cancer (for urology)
manual.s3FilterMode: Display mode — All, Combinations only, Individual only
manual.s3FavDesc: Click the star icon on a drug card to mark it as a favourite. Favourites appear in a dedicated section at the top of the overview.
manual.s3FavExport: Export: You can export all your favourites as a single PDF via the "Export favourites" button. You can choose whether dosing and side effects are included.
manual.s3MostUsedDesc: Click the lightning icon to mark a drug as "most used" (max. 8). These appear as shortcuts on the home page.
manual.s3OrderDesc: Via the "Adjust order" button in the toolbar you can reorder drugs by dragging them (drag-and-drop). Your personal order is saved and preserved across all devices.
manual.s3OrderReset: Reset: You can reset your custom order to the default order via the "Reset default" button in edit mode.
manual.s4Intro: Click on a drug card to view the full information.
manual.s4OverviewDesc: The detail page contains several tabs:
manual.s4Tab1: Overview — Indications, mechanism of action, reimbursement status, route of administration
manual.s4Tab3: Side effects — Common and serious side effects
manual.s4Tab4: Contraindications — When the drug should not be used
manual.s4Tab6: References — Links to external sources and studies
manual.s4FolderDesc: From the detail page you can generate a patient leaflet — a printable information sheet for the patient.
manual.s4FolderStep6: Click "Generate leaflet" to see the preview.
manual.s4FolderStep7: Print via the print button or download as PDF.
manual.s4FolderTip: The leaflet automatically includes your hospital's logo and brand colour, as well as the contact details of the selected staff members.
manual.s5Intro: OncoInfo offers two ways to quickly access your frequently used drugs:
manual.s5Fav2: Appear as a separate section on the overview page
manual.s5Most2: Appear as shortcuts on the home page
manual.s5Both: Both are saved to your account and available on all your devices.
manual.s6Intro: OncoInfo has different roles, each with their own permissions:
manual.s6ViewerDesc: Can view drugs, manage favourites, generate patient leaflets and adjust the order.
manual.s6DoctorDesc: Same permissions as Viewer. Name automatically appears as treating physician on patient leaflets.
manual.s6PharmacistDesc: Can additionally edit drug information and customise patient leaflets via the admin panel.
manual.s6AdminDesc: Full access to the admin panel: user management, activity log, add/edit drugs and adjust the global order.
manual.s6RoleVisible: Your current role is visible in the navigation bar next to your name. Hover over the badge for an overview of your permissions.
manual.s7Intro: Admins and pharmacists have access to the admin panel via the user menu (click your profile icon → Management).
manual.s7UsersDesc: Manage the users of your hospital:
manual.s7Users1: Create new users (with automatic welcome email)
manual.s7AuditDesc: View an overview of all changes to drugs, patient leaflets and users within your hospital.
manual.s7AddDesc: Search and import new therapies/treatment regimens via the "Add therapy" button. The system searches medical databases and adds the drug to your library.
manual.s7AutoDesc: If activated for your hospital, the system can automatically detect new therapies and updates based on scientific publications.
manual.s8Lang1: The interface language is automatically set based on your hospital's default language.
manual.s8Lang2: You can change the language on the login page (flags at the bottom) or via the user menu (super admins).
manual.s8Lang3: The patient leaflet can be generated in a different language than the interface language.
manual.s8Lang4: Medical terms are automatically translated where possible.
manual.s9Intro: OncoInfo works entirely in the browser and is optimised for desktop, tablet and smartphone. You can log in on multiple devices simultaneously with the same account.
manual.s9Item1: Your favourites, most used regimens and custom order are synchronised via your account.
manual.s9Item2: Changes on one device are visible on the other device after a refresh.
manual.s9Item3: The app is available as a Progressive Web App (PWA) — you can "add to home screen" on your smartphone.
manual.s10Q1: How do I change my password?
manual.s10A1: After your first login you will be automatically asked to change your password. After that, your admin can reset the password if needed.
manual.s10A2: Your hospital may not have all disciplines activated. Contact your admin to enable disciplines.
manual.s10Q3: How do I print a patient leaflet?
manual.s10A3: Go to a drug detail page → Patient leaflet → Select physician and nurse → Generate → Use the print or download button.
manual.s10Q4: Can I install the app on my phone?
manual.s10A4: Yes! Open OncoInfo in your mobile browser and choose "Add to home screen" (via the share menu on iOS or the browser menu on Android).
manual.s10Q5: Are my favourites saved when I log out?
manual.s10A5: Yes. Favourites and most used regimens are saved to your account and available when you log in again, even on a different device.
manual.disclaimerText: OncoInfo is intended solely as an informational tool and is not a medical device (MDR 2017/745). The content may contain errors and should not be used as the sole basis for clinical decisions. Always consult your treating physician or pharmacist.
